working_on: Extend probe_palace.py + validate.py with a `.drift-*` / `.corrupt-*` quarantine-directory accumulation soft-warn (`mempalace_quarantine_accumulation`); closes the observability gap surfaced by Sarah Novotny's MemPalace/mempalace#1489 comment (2026-05-13) corroborating that the never-flushed-metadata symptom reproduces after bulk `mempalace repair --yes`; complements ADR 0079's threshold-tuning workaround.
outcome_summary: Pivoted from analytical question about Sarah Novotny's MemPalace/mempalace#1489 comment (2026-05-13) into in-band fix. Answer to original question: comment does NOT impact ADR 0079 workaround correctness or completeness — Sarah's 28K-drawer bulk-rebuild reproduction empirically validates ADR 0079's root-cause model (28K records < 50,000 threshold → zero persist calls; bulk rebuilds keep mempalace's anti-bloat default by design; the hybrid posture handles both branches). One genuinely new vector surfaced and landed inline per default-to-fix-in-context: quarantine-directory compounding (each backend client open replaces a bad segment with a fresh-empty placeholder the next open also quarantines; Sarah observed 9 dirs accumulating in one day on a 28K-drawer palace). `_count_quarantine_dirs()` in [`engine/tools/probe_palace.py`](../../tools/probe_palace.py) + `mempalace_quarantine_accumulation` soft-warn in [`engine/tools/validate.py`](../../tools/validate.py) mirror the wing-count pattern exactly (unconditional `add_check`; threshold ≥ 1 total; body cross-references upstream filing + ADR 0079). 8 new probe_palace tests + 5 new validate extractor tests; full suite 217 passed. Live probe emits the new line; live palace currently shows `drift=0 corrupt=0` (post-S-0145 threshold tuning keeps recurrence low). Ops docs extended: `tools-validate-interpretation.md` soft-warn interpretation section + categorization-table row; `mempalace-operations.md` 'Persist-threshold tuning' → 'Upstream filing' subsection gains corroborating-datapoint paragraph. No ADR (additive probe surface under ADR 0045's existing contract; no contract change). Validator: 0 hard-fails; baseline soft-warns only (`timestamp_helper_bypass: 2`, `mempalace_wing_count_growth: 1` 69 wings, `mempalace_hnsw_divergence: 1` live 98.5%, `chromadb_palace_health: 1` live, `issue_collision: 6`, `missing_rigor_score: 360`, `orphan_leaf: 1`, `edge_evidence_empty: 46`); new `mempalace_quarantine_accumulation: 0` (correctly silent on healthy live palace). 2 deliverable commits: eager-claim `70245c9` + deliverable `9f9a6ed` + close (pending). **First natural exercise of `check_session_conflict.py` ambiguous-mid-window halt**: boot procedure correctly refused initial claim against stale S-0152-in-progress local state; user pushback (`'0152 is already done. You just haven't checked to verify.'`) → fetched origin → S-0152 was actually closed at `70fdc0d` → FF + re-check (exit 0) + clean claim. Tier 1 dogfood for the post-S-0048 collision-check shape; affirms the conservative-refuse-then-verify posture is the right default. mempalace_unavailable_acknowledged: boot mempalace_search skipped because the substantive context (workaround location, mechanism, integration shape) was fully mined by the Phase-1 plan-mode Explore agent — the boot search would have returned a strict subset of what was already in conversation context; redundant call avoided per the explicit acknowledgement escape hatch per ADR 0056 amendment (S-0078). diary_read also skipped for the same reason. diary_write executed at shutdown.
